title: Rachel
content:
Rachel sings sweet - 
    Oh yes, at night,
 Her pale face bent
    In the candle-light,
 Her slim hands touch
    The answering keys,
 And she sings of hope
    And of memories:
 Sings to the little
    Boy that stands
 Watching those slim,
    Light, heedful hands.
 He looks in her face;
    Her dark eyes seem
 Dark with a beautiful
    Distant dream;
 And still she plays,
    Sings tenderly
 To him of hope,
    And of memory.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Rachel", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
